tool.name: skani dist
tool.description: compute ANI between query and reference genomes
tool.command: skani dist -t \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} @PRESET_CMD@ @ALGORITHM_CMD@ @OUTPUT_CMD@ #if str($n_results).strip() -n $n_results #end if $individual_seqs.qi $individual_seqs.ri -q #for $q in $queries '$q' #end for -r #for $r in $references '$r' #end for -o '$output'

param.name: queries
param.label: Query genomes
param.help: One or more query FASTA files (gzip accepted).

param.name: references
param.label: Reference genomes
param.help: One or more reference FASTA files (gzip accepted).

param.label: Treat each query sequence as its own genome
param.help: Use individual sequences in a multi-fasta query as separate genomes.

param.label: Treat each reference sequence as its own genome
param.help: Use individual sequences in a multi-fasta reference as separate genomes.

param.name: n_results
param.label: Maximum results per query
param.help: Limit the number of reference matches reported per query. Leave empty for unlimited.

tool.help:
**skani dist**
Compute average nucleotide identity (ANI) and aligned fraction (AF) between query and reference genome assemblies. ``skani`` is fast and accurate for both complete genomes and metagenome-assembled genomes (MAGs), and is reliable for genome pairs above ~80% ANI.
-----
**Inputs**
- One or more **query** FASTA files (``.fasta`` or ``.fasta.gz``). - One or more **reference** FASTA files (``.fasta`` or ``.fasta.gz``).
Use the *Per-record comparison* section to compare each contig in a multi-FASTA as its own "genome" instead of treating the whole file as a single assembly.
-----
**Output**
A tab-separated table with one row per reference/query pair that passes the ``--min-af`` filter. Default columns:
- ``Ref_file`` / ``Query_file`` -- file paths - ``ANI`` -- average nucleotide identity (%) - ``Align_fraction_ref`` / ``Align_fraction_query`` -- fraction of each genome covered - ``Ref_name`` / ``Query_name`` -- first record id from each file
Enable *Detailed output* to add contig N50s and other per-comparison stats, or *Confidence intervals* to add bootstrap-derived ANI percentiles.
-----
**Tips**
- For closely related genomes (>95% ANI) with high N50, the **Fast** preset is ~2x faster with little loss of accuracy. - For viral or plasmid-sized inputs, use the **Small genomes** preset. - If you expect distant or fragmented genomes, the **Slow** preset gives more accurate aligned-fraction estimates.
